extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the bunch sprint after BARBIN Enrico preserved KOM jersey by contesting mid-race mountains points from the breakaway.
tactical_patterns: BARBIN Enrico immediately joined break to defend KOM jersey rather than conserve energy, revealing category jersey tactics override stage ambitions.
BOIVIN Guillaume targeted both intermediate sprint and KOM from break, showing Israeli Academy prioritizes visibility over realistic GC threats.
VIVIANI pushed rival MODOLO to barriers in intermediate sprint, establishing psychological dominance before final sprint confrontation.
Bahrain-Merida paced peloton at significantly above approach pace through middle section, fragmenting sprint trains before final positioning phase.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: Won back-to-back stages and demonstrated tactical aggression by forcing MODOLO wide in intermediate sprint before controlling finale.
rider_signals.BARBIN Enrico: neutral: Successfully defended KOM jersey from breakaway but revealed willingness to expend significant energy for minor classification prize.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: Finished third in sprint despite positioning support, confirming speed deficit to VIVIANI in straight finishing scenarios.
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: negative: Challenged VIVIANI in intermediate sprint but intimidated to barriers, then launched final sprint too early and faded.
rider_signals.FRAPPORTI Marco: neutral: Won KOM from breakaway but two-day effort yielded only one classification point, exposing weak break selection calculus.
